channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Hit VTuber Ironmouse Announced As FFXIV FanFest Anaheim Guest For Special Panel Appearance
item.content:encoded:
With the first of 2026’s Final Fantasy Fan Festivals arriving next week in Anaheim, the latest announcement unveils Ironmouse, arguably one of the world’s most famous VTubers, as a guest panelist. 

Ironmouse is described in the official announcement as an avid FFXIV player who opts to play during her off-stream downtime. 

It appears Ironmouse will appear as part of Saturday’s “From Tacos to Tenders: Serving up the Lyrics of FFXIV,” which, as outlined in the weekend’s schedule (including free panel streaming), brings on the original localizer and FFXIV soundtrack lyricist Koji Fox “as he rants, raves, and shares stories about bringing some of your favorite songs to life.” 

The Ironmouse announcement claims that they “felt this created the perfect opportunity to invite her to join in on a portion of Koji Fox's panel discussing the creation of FINAL FANTASY XIV music and lyrics! We should warn her that Koji can be a bit... excitable when meeting his (virtual) idols.” 

Plus, it seems they’re teasing a possible surprise performance, as they encourage fans to light up their idol glowsticks—which are canon in FFXIV via Little Ladies’ Day, so don’t worry about breaking immersion too much. This all isn’t long before The Primals’ concert later that night, which is available at home as a paid stream, so we hope Koji Fox won’t be trying to scratch his voice too much during his own panel.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/hit-vtuber-ironmouse-announced-as-ffxiv-fanfest-anaheim-guest-for-special-panel-appearance-2000137826
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Albion Online's Latest Dev Talk Takes Us Behind the Controller and UI Changes in Time for Next Week's Xbox Launch
item.content:encoded:
Now that Radiant Wilds is live, the Albion Online team is turning to the upcoming Xbox launch, which happens next week, on April 21st. In a new dev talk, co-creative director Michael Schwahn dives into how the team improved the controller support on all platforms, and the UI in time for the first console release.

The launch on Xbox Series X|S is the first console release, and this took a lot of planning. They call this an evolution, and decided to create a complete cross-platform, cross-progression experience. If you already have an account, and decide to plan Xbox next week, you can link that account and continue on any platform whenever you want.

Their goal was to  let everyone play on the same servers, freely switch platforms as you want, and to get the full experience being walled off in any way.  you can also just start on Xbox when it launches and if you want to use cross-platform features later, link your account and you'll be set.

While controller support did exist before this, Schwahn says that the team examined all of it in efforts to improve controller play, and create a more intuitive, reliable, scheme. This also meant UI redesigns, to put the most-accessed menu options accessible in a way that is quick and easy. The radio menu is also overhauled with some quick actions to reduce the need to navigate through different menu chains, and even allow for quick communication without the need to type. 

Many improvements to controllers already landed in Radiant Wilds, simply because they offer more options for everyone that wants to play. You can hit play above to get a peek at what the Xbox experience will look like, and read the accompanying devblog with screenshots.

If you are looking to play Albion Online on Xbox if you have a Game Pass subscription and there are also some launch goodies, for both new and returning players, including a few days of Premium, emotes, and more.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/albion-onlines-latest-dev-talk-takes-us-behind-the-controller-and-ui-changes-in-time-for-next-weeks-xbox-launch-2000137825
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
